title: PacBio Announces Third Quarter 2023 Financial Results
text: PacBio reported Q3 2023 revenue of $55.7 million, representing a 72% increase from $32.3 million in Q3 2022. Instrument revenue was $34.7 million, up 204.4% year-over-year, primarily from continued momentum of Revio systems. Consumables revenue was $16.9 million, up 4.9% from prior-year quarter. Service and other revenue totaled $4.1 million, down 14% year-over-year. Gross profit was $17.9 million with a 32% margin, down from 42% margin in Q3 2022. Net loss was $66.9 million, improved from $77.0 million loss in Q3 2022. Net loss per share was $0.26 versus $0.34 in Q3 2022. Cash and investments totaled $767.8 million as of September 30, 2023. PacBio shipped 52 Revio sequencing systems in Q3, bringing the installed base to 129 Revio systems as of September 30. CEO Christian Henry noted the company continued to drive Revio adoption and exceeded $50 million in quarterly revenue for the first time in company history. Key achievements included record consumable revenue with Revio products exceeding legacy Sequel II/IIe consumables, first Onso system shipments with positive customer reception, launch of WGS Variant Pipeline computational workflow, introduction of PacBio Capital financing program, and appointment of David Meline as Board member. The company revised 2023 revenue forecast upward, estimating between $195 million and $200 million, marking a 52% to 56% increase from 2022.
